evals.prompt: Write a TypeScript function that takes an array of users (each has: id, name, email, role, active boolean). Return an object where keys are role names and values are arrays of active users sorted by name. Make it production quality.
evals.expected_output: Uses filter + map + reduce (or groupBy), no for/while loops, no mutation (no push/sort in-place), pure function returning new data, uses const not let

assertions.name: no-for-or-while-loops
assertions.description: Code must not contain for, while, or do...while loops

assertions.name: no-mutating-methods
assertions.description: Code must not use mutating array methods: push, pop, splice, sort, reverse

assertions.name: uses-declarative-array-methods
assertions.description: Code must use at least one of filter, map, or reduce

assertions.name: pure-core-function
assertions.description: Main transformation function is pure — takes array param, returns new data

evals.prompt: I need a simple User class in TypeScript. It should have properties for id, name, email and methods to updateEmail(newEmail) and updateName(newName). Also a method to serialize to JSON.
evals.expected_output: Rejects OOP approach, uses plain interface/type with standalone pure functions returning new objects (spread), no class/this/extends, no mutation of input

assertions.name: no-class-keyword
assertions.description: Code must not contain the 'class' keyword

assertions.name: no-this-keyword
assertions.description: Code must not use the 'this' keyword

assertions.name: no-void-mutations
assertions.description: Update functions must return a value, not void

assertions.name: uses-spread-for-update
assertions.description: Update functions must use object spread to return new instances

assertions.name: has-plain-interface-or-type
assertions.description: Code must define a plain User interface or type

evals.prompt: Write a TypeScript function that fetches a user config from an API endpoint, parses the JSON response, and handles all error cases (network failure, invalid JSON, missing required fields). The function should be callable from a React component.
evals.expected_output: Uses Result/Either pattern (discriminated union) for error handling, isolates side effects (fetch) to edge, core parsing logic is pure, no throw for control flow, no try/catch wrapping business logic

assertions.name: has-discriminated-union-result
assertions.description: Code must define a discriminated union Result type (ok: true/false pattern)

assertions.name: no-throw-in-validation
assertions.description: Validation functions must not throw for control flow

assertions.name: parse-returns-result-not-throws
assertions.description: JSON parse function must return a Result, not throw to caller

assertions.name: fetch-isolated-to-edge
assertions.description: Only the main fetch function contains the fetch call

assertions.name: no-for-loop-in-validation
assertions.description: Validation logic uses declarative style (map/find/every), not for/while loops

evals.expected_output: Uses immutable updates with spread operator at each nested level, returns new object, no direct property mutation, isolates Date.now() side effect or passes timestamp as parameter

assertions.name: no-direct-property-mutation
assertions.description: Code must not directly assign to nested properties on existing objects

assertions.name: uses-spread-for-nested-update
assertions.description: Code must use spread operator at each nested level

assertions.name: no-let-binding
assertions.description: Code must not use let — prefer const

assertions.name: side-effect-isolated
assertions.description: Date.now() is isolated — passed as parameter or called at the edge
